medications.indication_rationale: NSAIDs are first-line for axSpA flares and are particularly effective in inflammatory back pain, taken with food if no contraindication.
medications.dose_route_frequency: e.g. naproxen 500 mg PO with food BD (adult, if no contraindication)

medications.indication_rationale: Adjunct/alternative analgesia if NSAIDs are contraindicated.
medications.dose_route_frequency: paracetamol 1 g PO up to QID as needed (adult)

medications.indication_rationale: Not indicated for inflammatory back pain; poor efficacy and adverse effects.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Stand and walk for ~2 minutes every hour, do gentle pain-free pelvic tilts, use heat, and avoid sitting longer than ~45 minutes — do NOT bed-rest.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Inflammatory back pain improves with movement; rest worsens it.

patient_education_points.point: This is a flare of your inflammatory back condition (axial spondyloarthritis), not a slipped disc or nerve damage — your leg strength, sensation and bladder/bowel control are all normal.
Unlike a mechanical back, inflammatory back pain gets WORSE with rest and BETTER with movement — take an anti-inflammatory with food, use heat, and move gently and often (about 2 minutes every hour).
Call 000 or go to ED immediately if you lose control of your bladder or bowels, develop numbness around your groin/buttocks or back passage, or your legs become weak or give way.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Flare control, continuous NSAID vs biologic therapy, rheumatology referral.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Cauda equina features, progressive leg weakness, or shooting leg pain with numbness.

must_include_items: Explicitly screen cauda equina (saddle anaesthesia, bladder/bowel, leg weakness) and severe radiculopathy
Advise NSAIDs + frequent gentle movement (not bed rest) + heat
Explain inflammatory back pain improves with movement
errors_of_omission: No cauda equina screen or safety-net
errors_of_commission: ED over-triage of an inflammatory flare with a normal neuro screen
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: T2 case: passing REQUIRES an explicit cauda equina screen + a firm safety-net, plus movement-based management. Skipping the cauda equina screen or advising bed rest are failures.
